calendar.matters:
MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Feb-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF AMERICAN ACCOUNTABILITY FOUNDATION's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Petitioner American Accountability Foundation's Motion to Admit Counsel Pro Hac Vice, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Counsel Daniel W. Tilleman may appear as counsel pro hac vice for moving party in this action.

Petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and must drop a hard copy of the proposed order in Detp. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief Under The California Public Records Act
Off Calendar Feb-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Stephen Duvernay, Esq. Daniel Tilleman, Esq. 916-447-4900 Benbrook Law Group for Plaintiff.
Raymond Cardozo, Esq. (In-person) 415-659-5990 Corinne Fierro, Esq. 213-457-8145 Reed Smith LLP for Defendant.
Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293, 415-274-9977, calendar-veritext.com, reported.

ARGUED. Mr. Duvernay, counsel for Plaintiff requests to adopt the tentative ruling. Mr. Cardozo contests the tentative ruling and presents arguments. The Court shares its comments.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF AMERICAN ACCOUNTABILITY FOUNDATION's Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief Under The California Public Records Act.

Taken Under Submission.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293, 415-274-9977, calendar-veritext.com, reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief Under The California Public Records Act

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF AMERICAN ACCOUNTABILITY FOUNDATION's Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief Under The California Public Records Act.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Judge Harold Kahn, who is the judge assigned to department 302 for all hearings on January 16, 2025, is recusing himself from all matters in this case. The hearing is continued to January 28, 2025 to be heard by a judge other than Judge Kahn.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/HEK)
